L1.area: Request handling and server-side state management

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a request parameter containing serialized state data is deserialized without proper validation. An attacker can craft a malicious serialized payload that, when processed, results in remote code execution.

L3.area: Request handling and server-side state management
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a request parameter containing serialized state data is deserialized without proper validation. An attacker can craft a malicious serialized payload that, when processed, results in remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The extractState method deserializes user-supplied data from the _state request parameter using PHP's unserialize() without proper validation, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code via crafted serialized payloads.
